entry.title: SuccessFactors Role Based Permission Clean Up
entry.content:
We are in the initial stages of reviewing our SuccessFactors Role Based Permission (RBP) roles to "clean up" duplicate or obsolete roles. What is the best practice approach for old roles? Should we delete them or just rename them so they are not used in the future? Any guidance is greatly appreciated.

Thanks,

Lisa

entry.title: Missing tab in SOAMANAGER
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

We're currently activating our Inbound for EDI, and while we run the SOAMANAGER, we didn't see the "Application and Scenario Communcation Tab". 

Is there any updates or changes for this tab name in SOAMANAGER? or is there a guide to add this tab via authorization in SU01 to be visible this in SOAMANAGER?

BTW, currently we're using S4 HANA Private Cloud Edition 2020.

Thank you in advance!
-Jaspher

entry.title: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if it does not affect compatibility?
entry.content:
I am working on a task to add a new field and hide existing fields from an "Assigned to" search help in Fiori App F5104A (Perform Maintenance Jobs). I have extended the "Assigned To" search help view to add a new field for division. In addition, in order to hide existing fields, I need to add a metadata extension. However, I get an error in the metadata extension shown below.  When I attempt to add the annotation to the standard base view, I get the warnings shown below as well. According to my findings it seems that if I do not modify or remove any existing parameters, associations, name changes, then anything else should not affect incompatibility. Is it ok to add the annotation "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true" to the standard base view in order to proceed with the metadata extension? The standard CDS view that has been extended and annotated is I_PersWrkAgrmtSrchHelp. 

"Assigned To" field in Fiori App F5104A:

"Assigned To" search help with division added:

 

Error in metadata extension:

 

Warning in standard CDS view when attempting to add "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true".

4

Link to the release contract (Use System-Internally (C1)) mentioned in the above warning:

Release Contracts | ABAP Keyword Documentation

I would appreciate the feedback in order to help my team and I move forward with this.

 

Thank you!

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content:
Hello @dean_hinson2 

Below, i summarized the solution

Required Task

What to do

ALRG03 not finding legal regs

Check assignment in Manage Legal Control Settings

Legal regulation not active

Confirm it's scoped and assigned

Country/country group missing

Assign via Configure Your Solution (SSCUI)

Testing

Use Check Compliance app or simulate a transaction

Scope item required

Ensure 1Z3 is activated

 

SAP Help References

  • SAP Help – Legal Control (Public Cloud)
  • Scope Item 1Z3 – Legal Control

With kind regards

Chujma

entry.title: Re: Physical quantity and available quantity in EWM
entry.content: Thanks for response.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud infrastructure is moving to SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: The migration process is underway. The plan is that customers will be migrated during the normal maintenance windows. Customers will need to wait their turn as we will be migrating based on a number of factors such as the data center provider and region, customer system size etc.

entry.title: Re: Coming Soon: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private (former SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Editi
entry.content: Is there already an ETA for S/4HANA Cloud Private Editon release navigator?

entry.title: Re: 🚀 Avoid Performance Pitfalls: Master Data Design Tips for SAP Convergent Charging
entry.content: Excellent points Nupur!

entry.title: Join Our Business Builders Roadshow | July 8 in Spanish!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to invite you to our Business Builders Roadshow, where we will be launching two new games on our platform!

Join us for our regional roadshow, hosted by SAP University Alliances and HEC Montréal on July 8th 17:00 CEST | 11:00 EDT in Spanish (Check out other dates and times here), where we will provide you with a hands-on preview of the new games:

  1. The Maple Heir, a business fundamentals scenario developed by Prof. Michael Bliemel,
  2. The Agent, a sports management scenario developed by Prof. Olivier Caya.

These games are perfect for students from school to university, making data visualization and data storytelling fun and easy to learn.

You’ll have the opportunity to play these games firsthand and experience the fun and learning they provide. You’ll also experience demos, regional faculty testimonials, and Q&A, providing a comprehensive taste of the new games.

Whether you are already familiar with Business Builders or are new to the community platform, this is a fantastic opportunity to discover how a gamified learning experience can help enhance classroom learning and develop SAP Analytics Cloud skills. By integrating Business Builders into your curriculum, you will be giving your students the chance to tackle real-world business challenges, empowering them to stand out in the job market.

And the best: Business Builders is free for educational use.

Register now and let’s shape the future of learning together!

See you there!

entry.title: Re: "PD Where Used" Button – A Chrome Extension for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: This is a great feature, would be useful where the address is static, for dynamic addresses this option would not work, such as determining through property.

entry.title: SAP HANA Backup to tape with Veeam-Plug-In
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte Damen und PERSON_NAME,

 

wir möchten unsere SAP HANA-DB künftig über die Backint-Schnittstelle und dem Veeam-Plug-In für SAP HANA sichern.
Für unser Test-System wurde der Prozess mittlerweile auch schon eingerichtet und funktioniert auch problemlos.

 

Aktuell wird das Backup über einen backup-Job in das lokale Veeam-Repository auf dem Backup-Server geschrieben.
Wir möchten das Backup künftig in einem zweiten Step jedoch auch noch auf ein Tape speichern.

 

Hierfür stehen gem. unseren Informationen folgende Optionen zu Verfügung: 

 

(1) Files to tape

 

(2) Backups to tape:

 

Da man hier die SAP-Backup-Jobs aber nicht auswählen kann, müsste man sich in der Job-Konfiguration für Variante „Backup Repositories“ auswählen. Dies setzt dann voraus, dass man die SAP HANA Jobs auf ein separates Repository laufen lässt und nur dieses sichert.

 

Können Sie uns sagen, welche Option gem. SAP die bessere wäre?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Backup to tape with Veeam-Plug-In
entry.content: SAP HANA backups to tape can be supported via third-party backup tools that are certified to use the Backint for SAP HANA interface. These tools can integrate with SAP HANA to perform backup and recovery operations, including transferring backups to tape. It is essential that the third-party tool's API implementation is certified by SAP and that you have a support contract with the tool vendor.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance order display blank screen in SAP Service and Asset Manager
entry.content: Hi Muhammad, Debug attached

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content: Hello Chujma, The LRREG is configured with Country Group and ExpCtl. = 4. Manage Legal Control Settings is not in GTSE4H. Control Settings for Legal Control is in GTSE4H and I don't see anything that would cause the issue I am encountering. Country Group is not missing. SSCUI transaction doers not exist in GTSE4H. Also, we are not on the Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Tutorial: Save and display text field more than 255 characters from Table Maintenance (SM30)
entry.content:
Hi arnabdatta3,

thank you for sharing this solution. I'm encountering an issue when inserting new rows: after some investigation, I found out that the vim_status for new rows should be neuer instead of aendern.

I slightly modified the code to handle this case as follows:

IF <fs_line_struc>-vim_action NE neuer.
  <fs_line_struc>-vim_action = aendern.
ENDIF.

However, after saving and navigating back, the system still prompts me to save again, as if the previous save wasn't recognized.

Please, let me now if you have any suggestions on how to fix this.

entry.title: Re: Unable to find any active legal regulations for deter...
entry.content: Hello Chujma, Also to cover bases, the legal regulations has both country group and individual coutnries assigned.

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Post tax payable - Vat return Poland - list of invoic...
entry.content:
Hi @Kamil14 

I hope you know that you can easily access FBL3N in SAP Public Cloud through Fiori Apps. Also you can easily search them in Fiori App Library with T Code. 

Below are details of FBL3N Fiori app in SAP Public Cloud.

App ID F2217- Display Line Items in General Ledger.
 
Please check and confirm.
 
Regards
Vijaykumar

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Ariba SLP with S/4HANA Public Cloud Using SAP Master Data Integration
entry.content:
Good to see a blog on new approach Karthikeyan_C. Appreciate it.

I have a question on Partition Supplier Replication from S4 Cloud to Ariba Buying. Not sure if you can suggest some process.

My scenario is we don't have SAP Variant Realms, so cannot go with this approach of SLP integration with S4 Cloud. Planning to use CIG+CPI and consume SAP_COM_0008 scope for Common Suppliers replication (Hope it will work). How about Partition Supplier replication ? Since we don't have any CIG Addon to generate the partition data, i believe we should go with Customization on S4 Cloud to generate these partition files using Developer 

Any suggestions on process or best approach for generating Partition data from S4 Cloud to Ariba B&I. 

Appreciate any suggestions.

entry.title: Re: Can I add an annotation to a standard SAP CDS view if...
entry.content:
Is it ok to add the annotation "@Metadata.allowExtensions: true" to the standard base view in order to proceed with the metadata extension? 

No. It's a change in a standard artifact. 

Have you tried using UI Adaptation to show and hide other fields? It seems feasible based on your request.

entry.title: Re: Report on suppliers' bank data
entry.content:
Hello AlicjaK. Welcome to the SAP Community. Your question is valid - but it lacks important details and it's assigned to the wrong tag. This is not a SAPUI5 question.

I highly suggest you to spend some time reading the following blog post: 
https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-blog-posts/asking-good-questions-in-the-scn-discussion-spaces-will-help-you-get-good/ba-p/12880734

Just to give some insights, I would add in your question:

- Which system and version you are using
- Where and how you have done your research
- Any tables/CDS views that might be helpful containing the data you need

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Test Case manual sequencing reorg fun...
entry.content: The benefit of Cloud at work here -> this enhancement has been delivered. There is now a means to indicate a sequence. KBA 3610413 has been updated to reflect this.

entry.title: Re: Condition Type GWLB for Intrastat
entry.content: Hi All, At the moment, we have the following customization in the system: Level Counter KArt Description From To Manu Obl Stat D ZwiSu 70 0 GRWR Border Crossing Value 20 X 75 0 GWLB Limit Value LB-Comp. X 79 0 Sum of Border Crossing 70 78 X C Values. The condition GRWR and the subtotal have the requirement '008'. The condition GWLB has the requirement '54' and the formula '60'. Level 79 has been assigned subtotal C so that only the total sum is passed as the border crossing value. How can I have the GRWR condition automatically included in an order and not select it manually?

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required: Restricting Receipt Stock Usage...
entry.content:
@Kiransonawane  , Can you share a bit more info?

From what I understand, here's a possible approach:
You can create the production order in your WIP warehouse, and once production is done and the order is closed, just transfer the finished goods to your FG warehouse. In the meantime, block your sales users from making any transactions directly from the WIP warehouse.

entry.title: Re: 🇲🇽 SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud (Mexico City, Mexico)
entry.content:
Bonjour, les gars.
I've registered for the SAP CodeJam event. I'm excited to participate in this session!

entry.title: Is there a BTP service or API to track user login/logout times and session duration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working on a project in SAP BTP and would like to know if there is any built-in service or API that can provide information such as:

  • When a user logged in

  • When they logged out

  • How long they stayed logged in (session duration)

This would help us monitor usage patterns and possibly audit access times.

Is there a service (like Audit Log, Identity Authentication, or another BTP capability) that offers this kind of data? If so, how can it be accessed?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: TDS LINE ITEM NOT PICKING IN J1INCHLN
entry.content: Hi Rudra did you get any answer I am also facing same issue

entry.title: ORACLE_BASE to use when switching DB software home from SAP Classic to Oracle standard
entry.content:
I have installed a new /oracle/RDBMS/19.0.0 shared home on server on which an existing SAP classic user installation was done under /oracle/C11/19.0.0

Note 1521371 suggests the shared ORACLE_BASE should be /oracle/RDBMS on a shared home, but in Note 1915317, Step 7 "Chceck Oracle environment for consistency suggests that the ORACLE_BASE must be the same when logged in as the ora<sid> or oracle users.  

which is correct?

I actually installed the new shared home with ORACLE_BASE=/oracle, which is how we originally did the software install under the SAP Classic user setup.  Even though ORACLE_BASE under the SAP classic user (oraq11) was specified as /oracle, when logged in as the oraq11 user the ORACLE_BASE=/oracle/Q11.  So I thought I'd be ok to do the same.

Now I'm getting permissions issues when I try to run SBP bundle patching for the first time because it can't write to the cfgtoollogs directory.  It is set with permission 755.  On some other non-SAP oracle installations that I have the cfgtoollogs directory has 775.

I'm really confused as to which path to follow and what is expected, as SAP installations are different from a typical Oracle installation with which I'm familiar.

entry.title: Re: Warehouse Transfers
entry.content:
Hi @FilipeCorreia ,

Let’s say you want to transfer an item from Warehouse A to Warehouse B.
You can do a Goods Issue from Warehouse A using the actual cost, and then create a Goods Receipt in Warehouse B with the actual cost + any landed costs you want to apply.

Let me know if this works for you!

 In  app approve ATC Exemptions, the buttons aprove, reject and delete is enabled. Can you a help?

entry.title: Re: Limit sales order partner function number - taken onl...
entry.content:
Hi , In your partner detemination procedure, there is an option called "Not modifiable". By selecting  this option, the partner function becomes non-editable in the sales order. It will be automatically copied from the customer master and cannot be changed in the sales order . Please find screen shot in below.

.  Please check this option and let us know if you still see an issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 HANA approval using queries

Did you check the Approval Status Report? It should list all the approval stages for the specific document. Let me know what you see there!

Rearranging the toolbar is not allowed.  It only allows us to add a fragment, or extend with a controller. 

 

Do you have any other options?

Thank you!

Patrick

how can achieve that?
Please guide, As I am new to APIM.

Ikram

entry.title: Business transformation management: Where do I start?
entry.content:
In the Experience Center at SAP Sapphire in Orlando and Madrid, we presented our full set of business transformation management capabilities. We did it by walking attendees through a scenario in which a company wanted to take full advantage of the SAP Business Suite, SAP BDC, and SAP Business AI by transforming a core business process. 

In the scenario we laid out, the company starts by using the Business AI embedded in SAP Signavio Transformation Advisor to recommend process improvements. Subsequently, through Signavio’s integration with SAP LeanIX, the company discovers that phasing in a successor application to support the transformed processes would also be beneficial.  

As the analysis progresses, the company decides on a plan of action, leveraging Signavio’s analysis and mining solutions to quantify the value they seek to realize in their transformation. They then take advantage of Signavio’s transformation management capabilities to collaboratively initiate, plan, manage, and execute their planned initiatives. 

Since their envisioned transformation involves changes to their enterprise architecture, including extending SAP S/4HANA with Ariba, the company plans their enterprise architecture roadmap in LeanIX. They also document this architectural decision there and record the changes by synchronizing everything with SAP Cloud ALM.  

Finally, to facilitate employee enablement, the company uses WalkMe to track and analyze adoption as well as to provide employees with in-app guidance.   

The power of an integrated toolchain for business transformation management 

As the scenario illustrates, an integrated toolchain makes business transformation a whole lot easier. It makes it possible to smoothly move from insight to planning to action to analysis, drilling down into the most relevant details and connecting those details to the broader business context.  

For example, the toolchain described can reveal manual steps in key processes, help you establish that the culprit is a poor functional fit between the process and the underlying application, and then plan for its replacement. 

Once you’ve phased it in, you can manage adoption of that application while also confirming, on the process level, that your changes are really delivering the value your initial analysis suggested you could achieve. 

In both Orlando and Madrid, customers, prospects, and partners (and even some of our SAP colleagues!) were uniformly enthusiastic when we showed them what our business transformation management toolset, supported by AI from start to finish, could do. 

As SAP LeanIX product marketer, Seth Lippincott put it, “There was no ambiguity in the responses we got. No one said, ‘Meh.’ They all wanted to know more.” 

Which is not to say that no one had questions. In fact, quite the opposite was true. In fact, there was one question that came up again and again: “Where do I start?” 

The answer to that question, as with so many questions in the complex world of enterprise software and leading-edge technology, is: It depends! 

Option 1: Start with a focus on your enterprise architecture 

So, what does the answer to this question depend on? Well, it depends first and foremost on your starting point.  

Let’s say your situation is this. You are relatively new to the SAP universe, and you just purchased SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. You want to transform your business by streamlining your business processes and modernizing the underlying enterprise architecture. In this scenario, it will probably take less time to create an inventory of your applications and, using the reference architecture content in LeanIX, map these applications to your business capabilities.  

That simple step can be quite revealing, showing you quickly where you have redundancies (i.e., more than one application supporting the same business capability), where you have gaps (unsupported capabilities), and where, for example, what applications you have on premise and what you have in the cloud.  

With this information, supported where possible by WalkMe’s insights into application usage, you can begin rationalizing your landscape, retiring or replacing applications that deliver no value, and ensuring future support for your critical business capabilities.  

The beauty of this approach? You can use the budget freed up through rationalization to fund your transformation efforts going forward.  

It also means that, as your business process management practice matures, you can design your to-be process landscape around a consolidated and optimized set of applications.  

That it’s easier to seamlessly enable your employees on a simplified application landscape? Icing on the cake.  

Option 2: Start with a focus on process excellence 

The scenario we presented in the Experience Center started with sourcing and procurement processes. One good reason for this was that the customer in the story had already embarked on their RISE with SAP journey. Based on that, we could assume that the customer was an established SAP ERP customer and already had key solutions from LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe in place.  

In many ways, your business processes are your business; they are the way you do things. If you want to bring costs down or accelerate time to market, it just makes sense to start with this operational reality–in other words, to dig into what’s actually happening and focus on what you could improve, optimize, and transform.  

Process transformation can deliver substantial, measurable impact. It also represents the centerpiece of any true business transformation, which, after all, means “changing the way you do business.” For this reason, starting with processes can provide you with a very practical lens through which to view things when you then turn to the transformation of your enterprise architecture.  

If the search for ways to optimize your processes uncovers the need for a new application, for example, you automatically have the business context and rationale for it. This then allows you to make the case and even quantify the value of the recommended architectural change. 

Option 3: Start wherever you can 

As we said above, where you start with transformation depends. It depends on what type of transformation you want to pursue. It depends on the solutions you have in place. And it depends on your organization’s capacity for transformation, or, putting it another way, how much transformation your organization is capable of.  

If your organization has developed a mature business process management practice, if you have an established enterprise architecture practice, if digital adoption is built into the way you use technology, then you have what we call a robust transformation capability. If that’s the case, you can start wherever you think you can generate the most value. 

That might mean that you start in several places at once. You can work on transforming business processes, modernizing your architecture, and enabling your entire organization to incorporate AI in every workflow. So long as the solutions you use are well integrated, and you coordinate your plans and roadmaps, you can be opportunistic when it comes to transformation.  

The challenge at this level of maturity is not figuring out where to start, but how best to orchestrate your efforts so they reinforce and amplify each other.  

When to start, not where to start, is the real question 

Where to start your transformation efforts can depend on myriad factors, as we have shown. 

When to start, however, only depends on one thing: Will successful transformation make your business better, more innovative, more competitive, more valuable?  

If the answer is yes, start today.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification: One-Stop-Shop for Enteprise Architects and Transformation Consultants
entry.content: Thank you very much! This content will help me a lot in my certification journey. �

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP API Proxy to Work with Basic Authentication or OAuth 2.0
entry.content: @ikramMohammed  go to developer hub. Create APP, while creating you can check Custom Application Key & Secret  and enter your own Username and Password.  Refer below screenshot

entry.title: Re: Message "Please input tax number in field Assignment...
entry.content:
Hello Siham

I hope you are doing well .

While the specific cause isn't always immediately clear, and some SAP messages (like those in the FIN_SUB_VAL class) are sometimes defined as "self-explanatory" by SAP without providing further details, this particular error points to a mandatory tax number input in the "Assignment" field.

If you are encountering this, here's what you should consider:

  • Recent Changes: Even if you haven't "touched the customizing" yourself, sometimes system updates or other configurations can trigger new validation rules.
  • Business Partner Master Data: Ensure the tax numbers for your suppliers (and potentially your own company code) are correctly maintained in the Business Partner master data. This is often the root cause of such errors.
  • Configuration Activities:
    • Check relevant configuration activities related to invoice verification and tax settings in "Manage Your Solution" in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. There might be a specific setting that has become mandatory or is now being enforced.
    • Look for activities related to tax determination, withholding tax, or country-specific tax requirements.

I hope it helps if not, please consider to be opening a support case os we can check further.

Best regards 

Fabio

entry.title: Re: "PD Where Used" Button – A Chrome Extension for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi @Vijay121, 

It supports only Cloud Foundry systems for now, if you are trying on Neo system you will get that error. Also you should be on the Integration Suite system, not on CPI.  

Your url should look like: xxx-is-dev.integrationsuite-cpi026.cfapps.eu10-002.hana.ondemand.com
It shouldn't be like: xxx-is-dev.it-cpi026.cfapps.eu10-002.hana.ondemand.com

If the problems are not these, we can check it again.

entry.title: BI 2025
entry.content:
Hello:

I understand that BI 2025 will no longer support UNV.  We have started planning to upgrade to BI 2025. I have some questions. 

1) Will I still be able to at least open the Webi reports pointing to UNV in BI 2025? I understand we may not be able to refresh which is fine. 

2) Is there a way to audit and identify what reports are pointing to UNV especially Personal and Inbox documents? Once identified is there a way to get rid (bulk delete) of the old webi reports that use UNV and not needed? 

3) What tools are everyone using to repoint the Webi reports to UNX? We are looking into Workflow Assistant tool. Looks like we can only repoint Public reports using Workflow Assistant tool and not Personal docs.

Thank you all in advance!

entry.title: Problem in CK11N and Cost Component
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have set up a cost component in OKTZ with a secondary account (Type 43) assigned

I have also charged its value in Cost Center for KP26
I perform the cost calculation with CK11N and the value calculated for
the activity is correct, but I don't understand why I don't get a separate line for
this component in the component view, even though the calculation is performed?
I'm attaching the result.
If anyone can help me, I'd appreciate it in advance.

Thanks all.

Mariano

 

 
 
.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning - Outlook Integration to book...
entry.content: I'm not sure if this is an approach you'd like to try but we have just started experimenting with creating LMS Users for our Outlook meeting rooms (ie. User Last Name = Meeting Room #1) .  We register the room (just like any other user) and as long as the room has an Outlook email address it seems to be working.  When the room "user" is registered it receives an Outlook invite which books the room.  We had our IT group involved in this to make sure that the Outlook settings for the meeting room accepted the LMS invite. We ask our admins who are creating classes to set the min/max participants with an extra space for the room.  This also uses up an Functional User license so you might want to take that into consideration depending on the number of rooms you have.

entry.title: Re: How many customers successfully implemented or migrat...
entry.content: I started to upgrade but ran into an error abut Administrator ID/PWD is not correct even though the pwd is correct. I have a ticket open with SAP related to this issue.

entry.title: Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model: Introduction – Chart your Course (1 of 5)
entry.content:
Today's logistics environment can feel like constantly navigating through a storm. Supply chain disruptions, unexpected market volatility, ongoing labor challenges, and the relentless pressure of customer expectations... these make even thinking about process and performance improvement more challenging when most of your energy is going into trying to ride out the storm. How do you find your way not just to calmer waters, but to rise to a level of operational excellence?

The journey begins with understanding where you are right now.

Drawing on insights from industry experts, including collaboration with Adrian Gonzalez of Adelante SCM, the latest Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model offers a powerful framework to help you chart your course to logistics excellence. The model serves as a simple yet essential tool for self-assessment and planning for progress in transportation management and collaboration across your logistics ecosystem. The model delves into key areas of your logistics capabilities, helping you pinpoint strengths and weaknesses, ultimately to identify areas where you can and must improve to compete in today’s global market.

Logistics leaders face myriad, intertwined internal and external challenges:

  • External Forces: Fragile supply chains, global conflicts/trade wars, rising energy prices, trade lane bottlenecks, workforce dynamics, technology acceleration, the race to carbon zero, and the cost of capital.
  • Internal Struggles: Manual, error-prone processes, a focus on firefighting, complex and outdated system landscapes, data silos leading to limited business insights, a lack of end-to-end process coverage, and missing freight visibility.

So, with day-to-day challenges, why take the time to apply a maturity model to your planning for process and performance improvement? It provides a structured way to assess your current state, benchmark your capabilities, define your target destination, and chart a strategic course to get there. It both digs into your day-to-day challenges, and rises above them to provide a bird’s eye view. It helps you connect specific operational improvements to real business outcomes like reducing costs, enhancing agility, and boosting customer satisfaction. It supports strategic thinking, with tactical application into your capabilities and competencies. 

Let’s break-down how this maturity model will help to:

  • Assess your current state: It helps you understand where you truly stand across those five critical dimensions. This self-assessment is the crucial first step to identifying specific areas where you need to grow.
  • Benchmark capabilities: You can see how your capabilities align with defined stages of maturity, offering context for your current position and highlighting what's possible.
  • Define your target state: The model helps you visualize the capabilities that define higher levels of maturity, allowing you to set a clear goal for where you aspire to be based on your strategic business objectives.
  • Chart a strategic path: It lays out the logical steps required to progress from your current level to your desired future state, guiding your "what's next" planning discussions.

Over the next four posts, we'll explore what this journey looks like at each stage, from basic foundational steps to the most advanced levels of operation. We'll discuss:

  • The starting line: Understanding and improving manual processes.
  • Gaining traction: Leveraging early digitalization for greater visibility.
  • Building momentum: Integrating processes for enhanced resilience.
  • Reaching the summit: Achieving autonomous orchestration and competitive advantage.

It’s not a race, but it is like climbing a mountain. And progressing along the model only matters when you maintain a focus on business value – when you determine that your logistics operations must not only improve to support business growth, but can transform into a competitive advantage for growth. 

Ready to see how the Supply Chain Logistics Maturity Model can guide your transformation? Stay tuned for the next post where we explore each stage of the journey, starting at level 1. 

Access the report now: 
https://www.sap.com/documents/2025/04/5a01c68e-027f-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.urc.html

Register for the Lessons from Leaders webinar with Borouge: 
https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/4966341/6D8C316165AD7FD3348CA5FFDF9ADFE3/6149724?partnerref=ForwardableEmail

entry.title: Instalación de Desktop Agent en SBPA.
entry.content:
Estimados.

Buenas tardes.

Junto con saludar, quería solicitar ayuda, ya que actualmente tenemos una instalación del desktop agent de Sap Build Process Automation, para lo cual lo utilizamos para la función de las automatizaciones del Workflow implementado, para la instalación del ejecutable y servicio me guie por la siguiente documentación:

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/install-desktop-agent-3-8c42e11b33824acc8273713f276589ea
https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/desktop-agent-service

En su momento se instaló el agente en el servidor a través de un usuario en específico, por lo cual, cuando el servidor realizaba las actualizaciones el agente se desconectaba debido a que solicitaba al usuario del servidor el ingreso de sesión, por dicho motivo, se realizó el cambio y se instaló en la máquina con el usuario de administrador, sin embargo, en la actualidad, cuando se realizan las actualizaciones automáticas del servidor también el agente se desconecta, dicho agente, ya se encuentra instalado también como servicio y dicho servicio cuando se visualiza el estado se encuentra en "running" , sin embargo, el agente aun así se desconecta. Esto me ocurre para el servidor de QA que tiene las actualizaciones de manera automática, no así, para el agente que se encuentra instalado en el servidor de PRD, ya que este servidor se encuentra con las actualizaciones programadas y el agente no se desconecta.

La versión que se está utilizando es la 3.32.58 y el modo se encuentra como "no asistido", si bien se entiende que existe una última actualización de dicho agente, no es compatible con la versión 1.48.34 del SDK ya deployado del proyecto. 

Quisiera saber si alguien ha instalado dicho agente en el servidor y como se puede estabilizar el estado de este.

Muchas gracias.

entry.title: Re: CDS views for clearing entry
entry.content:
Hello Dear Community member,

I hope you are doin well .

I know it' s been a long time that you have posted your question, but it can be helpful anyways.

In SAP S/4HANA, the Universal Journal Entry (ACDOCA) is the single source of truth for financial data. Clearing of open items (like invoices) is managed by linking the original open item to the clearing document within ACDOCA.

The key fields for determining clearing status and amount are generally:

  • ClearingDocument (AUGBL): This field contains the document number of the clearing document. If it's populated, the item is cleared.
  • ClearingDate (AUGDT): The date on which the item was cleared.
  • IsOpenItem (XBLNR): While not a direct clearing field, this can indicate if an item is still open. However, for cleared items, you'd primarily rely on ClearingDocument.
  • Amount fields: You'll need to consider fields like AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency (DMBTR) or AmountInTransactionCurrency (WRBTR) in conjunction with the clearing document to get the paid amount.

Standard CDS Views to Explore

While you've tried I_Journalentryitem and I_Journalentryitemcube, there are other standard CDS views that might provide better insights into clearing information, especially for accounts payable/receivable:

  • I_OperationalAcctgDocItem: As suggested in an SAP Community post, this CDS view often contains comprehensive operational accounting document item data, including clearing information. It's designed for more detailed operational reporting and might have the ClearingDocument and ClearingDate populated even for 'RV' document types where I_Journalentryitem doesn't. You can often find fields like ClearingDocument and ClearingPostingDate.
  • I_JournalEntryItemBasic or I_OperationalJournalEntryItem: These are more granular views that might expose additional fields related to clearing.
  • I_SupplierInvoice / I_SupplierInvoiceItem (for Accounts Payable): These views are specific to supplier invoices and often contain status information related to payment. While they might not directly show the clearing document, they could indicate if an invoice is "Free for Payment" or "Cleared".
  • I_CustomerInvoice / I_CustomerInvoiceItem (for Accounts Receivable): Similar to supplier invoices, these views provide details on customer invoices.
  • I_AccountingDocument / I_AccountingDocumentItem: These views provide a more generic view of accounting documents and might contain clearing information.

How to find relevant fields in standard CDS views:

Use the "View Browser" Fiori App in your S/4HANA Cloud system. This app is invaluable for searching for available CDS views, understanding their structure, and identifying relevant fields. You can search by keywords like "clearing," "invoice," "document," and then inspect the fields available in each view.

Building Custom CDS Views

If standard CDS views don't fully meet your needs, you can build custom CDS views using the "Custom CDS Views" Fiori App (Key User Extensibility). This is the recommended approach in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for creating analytical or transactional data models without ABAP coding.

Here's a conceptual approach to building a custom CDS view for your requirement:

Scenario: Determine if an invoice is cleared and get the clearing amount.

  1. Primary Data Source: Start with a reliable CDS view that contains your invoice data. I_OperationalAcctgDocItem is a strong candidate as it holds general ledger line item information, including clearing data. You could also start with I_JournalEntryItem if it contains your 'RV' documents, and then join to another view for clearing.
  2. Key Fields to Select from Primary Source:
    • CompanyCode
    • FiscalYear
    • AccountingDocument (your invoice number)
    • AccountingDocumentItem
    • DocumentType
    • ClearingDocument (Crucial for identifying cleared items)
    • ClearingDate
    • AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency (Original invoice amount)
    • DebitCreditCode (to distinguish between debits and credits)
    • FinancialAccountType (e.g., 'K' for vendor, 'D' for customer)
  3. Joining for Clearing Amount (If needed): If the ClearingDocument field is populated in your primary view, the original AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency (or AmountInTransactionCurrency) of the cleared item represents the paid amount.
    If you need to retrieve details about the clearing document itself (e.g., the total amount of the payment that cleared multiple invoices), you might need to join back to the same     I_OperationalAcctgDocItem (or I_JournalEntryItem) with an alias, linking on ClearingDocument to AccountingDocument. However, for a single invoice's clearing amount, the amount on the original invoice line item when ClearingDocument is populated is generally what you're looking for.
  4. Defining Calculated Fields (Measures):
    • IsCleared: You can create a calculated field (measure) to indicate if an invoice is cleared.
      • Expression: CASE WHEN ClearingDocument IS NOT NULL THEN 'X' ELSE '' END
      • Type: Boolean or String (if Boolean isn't supported for direct display)
    • ClearedAmount: This would be the AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency of the invoice item if it's cleared.
      • Expression: CASE WHEN ClearingDocument IS NOT NULL THEN AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency ELSE 0 END
      • Type: Amount
  5. Filters (Optional but Recommended):
    • Filter by DocumentType to focus on 'RV' invoices.
    • Filter by CompanyCode, FiscalYear, etc., to limit the data.
  6. Associations (If needed): If you need additional master data (e.g., vendor name, customer name, work package details), you can add associations to other relevant CDS views like I_Supplier (I_SupplierStdVH), I_Customer (I_CustomerStdVH), or project-related CDS views if your "work package" refers to a project.

Steps to create the Custom CDS View:

  1. Open "Custom CDS Views" App: Navigate to this app in your Fiori Launchpad.
  2. Create New: Click the "Create" button.
  3. Define Basic Info: Give it a meaningful name (starts with YY1_), label, and select the scenario (e.g., "Analytical Cube" or "General Purpose").
  4. Add Primary Data Source: Search for and add I_OperationalAcctgDocItem (or your chosen primary view).
  5. Select Elements: Choose the fields you need from the primary data source (e.g., CompanyCode, AccountingDocument, ClearingDocument, AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency, etc.).
  6. Add Associations (if necessary): If you need data from other views (e.g., I_Project for work package details if it's a WBS Element), add associations and define the join conditions.
  7. Create Calculated Fields: Go to the "Calculated Fields" tab and define IsCleared and ClearedAmount as described above.
  8. Define Filters: Apply filters as needed (e.g., DocumentType = 'RV').
  9. Preview: Use the "Preview" button to check the data output and ensure it's as expected.
  10. Publish: Once satisfied, publish the custom CDS view.

If your option would be taking the custom approach, please take in consideration the following aspects below:

Authorization: Ensure the user creating and consuming the custom CDS view has the necessary authorizations for the underlying standard CDS views and the data.
Performance: While CDS views are generally optimized, be mindful of complex joins or extensive calculations, especially on very large datasets. Test performance thoroughly.
Data Model Understanding: A good understanding of the underlying SAP S/4HANA Finance data model (especially ACDOCA and related tables) is crucial for selecting the correct base CDS views and fields.

Work Package Definition: Clarify what "work package" refers to in your system. Is it a WBS element, an internal order, a project task, or something else? This will dictate which additional CDS views (e.g., I_Project, I_WorkBreakdownStructureElement) you might need to associate.

Customer vs. Supplier Open Items: The clearing logic is similar for both, but ensure you are using the correct FinancialAccountType or appropriate standard CDS views (e.g., I_CustomerOpenItem vs. I_SupplierOpenItem) if you are building separate views for each.
Testing: Thoroughly test your custom CDS view with various scenarios, including cleared and uncleared 'RV' documents, to ensure it returns accurate results.

I hope it helps you all .

Best Regards

Fabio

entry.title: Unassign Purchasing Organization to Plant or Company Code
entry.content:
Hi all, 

As known, there are 3 types of POrg in SAP. 

1- Client level/Corporate-Wide/Centerlized POrg. Responsible to purchase for all company codes.

Assignment required: Assign POrg to all required plants under the different company codes. No CoCd assignment required in this type. 

2- Company Code Specific POrg. Responsible for buying material and service for all plants of a particular company code. 

Assignment required: Assing POrg to single CoCd + assign POrg to the required plants under this CoCd.

3- Plant-Specifc/Decentralized POrg. Responsible for buying materials and services for a single plant. 

Assignment required: Assign POrg to only single/one plant + assign POrg to CoCd of that plant.

-----

I wrote above forms of POrg to give you a picture of what I understood. 

My question is:

Is above relationships/assignments of the different types of POrg correct?
Also, for the client level/Centralized POrg assignment, I wrote the required assignment for this form is to assign POrg to all reuired plants. But what if I leave the existing POrg without assignment!!
I noticed that even though I leave the POrg without assignment, i can create PO. so,

Client level/Centralized  POrg assignment could be done by assigning POrg to all plants or leaving the POrg without assignment?
This is correct?
Sorry for typing this much of words. I hope somebody can assist me on this. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Italian Localisation: Payment Media Format F24 and pa...
entry.content:
Hello Karin,

I hope you are doing well .

We confirm that there is no standard procedure available for payment through form F24.

F24 payment media and methods have been somewhat distinct in how they are handled in SAP's various solutions for Italy. While Payment Media Forma F24"and "payment method F24" might not be available as a standard, out-of-the-box payment method within the general Accounts Payable payment programs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in the same way you might find for other payment methods (like SEPA).

Thanks for your question and sorry for doesn't have any other solution.

Best regards .

Fabio

entry.title: Document type field modification (BSART) in the purchase order
entry.content:
To modify the purchase order in transaction ME22N or the document type field (BSART) is being altered for purchase orders that are not in service category D.

For other purchase orders where the category field is not the service item it is being modified normally.
Please verify why purchase orders with category D Service Delivery are not altering the BSART field.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Columbus Q2 meetup
entry.content:
Today this event was organized successfully in Columbus at 1 pm EST. @Pawan , @manish1910  and @Vaibhav-Mishra  joined physically at the location, and Jaideep Bhati and @RajeevShah  joined online (google meet). The discussions were awesome.

7 new SAP Community logins are created with this group.

Amazing discussions on Master Data Management, Finance reports, AI/ML, SAP Silver partnership, etc. were done. The meeting concluded with a lunch sponsored by eBusiness Solutions Inc.

entry.title: Re: How to map bill of lading field from (post goods move...
entry.content:
Hello Cheney,

I hope you are doing well .

The standard mapping of the Bill of Lading from MIGO to MIRO in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is primarily for planned delivery costs. For other goods/services lines, the Bill of Lading is not automatically carried over. If a broader mapping is required, it generally necessitates custom development or utilizing alternative standard fields.

If your business requirement is to map the Bill of Lading to all lines on the supplier invoice, regardless of whether the PO contains planned costs, you would typically need to explore extensibility options in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This might involve:

Custom Fields: Creating a custom field in MIRO (supplier invoice) to store the Bill of Lading number.

Custom Logic (BAdIs): Implementing custom logic (Business Add-Ins or BAdIs) to copy the Bill of Lading from the MIGO document to your custom field in MIRO. This would be a development effort and would need to be carefully designed to ensure data consistency and avoid performance issues. 

Alternative Reference: As mentioned, consider if the Delivery Note field or another standard reference field in MIRO can fulfill your reporting or reconciliation needs for the Bill of Lading when it's not a planned delivery cost scenario.

By the way, for future questions related to Create Supplier Invoice , you can address to SAP S/4HANA Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement.

I hope it helps you.

Best regards

Fabio

entry.title: Reverse goods issue when TU is in completed status in EWM?
entry.content:
Hi guys,

Can we Reverse goods issue when TU is in completed status in EWM?

Thanks in anticipation of support.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification: One-Stop-Shop for Enteprise Architects and Transformation Consultants
entry.content: @Rodrigo_Cordeiro great to hear! Was there anything specific you found to be helpful? Everything in one place?

entry.title: Re: Configuration of 'invoice receipt date' in app 'Creat...
entry.content:
Hello TJ,

Ihope you are doing well .

You're right to notice that the "Invoice Receipt Date" field (REINDAT in the backend tables like BKPF or RBKP) might not be consistently visible in the "Create Supplier Invoice" app (F0859 or MIRO in some contexts) across all entities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

In SAP standard system field REINDAT is only available for certain country specific solutions (where it is a legal requirement) and for Public Sector Management (prompt payment act functionality is activated).

Only if such a country specification or PSM-PPA is active shall REINDAT field be active.

For the entity where it's appearing, check if it's assigned to a company code with a specific country/region configuration that mandates or makes this field visible for tax or reporting purposes (e.g., for VAT reporting in certain European countries like Czech Republic, as seen in search results, where "Invoice Receipt Date" can be reported instead of the "Tax Fulfillment Date").

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is continuously updated. It's possible that the "Invoice Receipt Date" was introduced or its visibility changed in a recent update. The entity where it's appearing might be on a slightly different version or have a specific feature activated.

 

Manage Your Solution (or Central Business Configuration if applicable): Look for configuration activities related to "Localizations," "Country-Specific Settings," or "Tax" for the relevant company codes.

App-Specific Settings: Sometimes, there are specific settings within the app's configuration (if available for "Create Supplier Invoice") that allow for field control based on country/region.

If it is not applicable to your country localization please stay turned  on SAP Roadmaps and Release Notes: Review the release notes for recent SAP S/4HANA Cloud updates to see if there are any mentions of changes to the "Create Supplier Invoice" app or the "Invoice Receipt Date" field.

 

SAP Support Portal/Knowledge Base: Search for SAP Notes or KBA (Knowledge Base Articles) related to "Invoice Receipt Date" and "Create Supplier Invoice" in S/4HANA Cloud.

By the way, for future questions please keep in mind that Create Supplier Invoice app belongs to MM - sourcing and procurement.

So you can use SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition Sourcing and Procurement.

I hope it helps you.

Best regards

Fabio

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Columbus Q2 meetup
entry.content: Glad to be part of very first SAP community meet from Columbus. Good to connect with sap experts and learn about sap latest updates and current innovations offering and how we can learn and contribute. We also discuss the current challenges faced by few attendees. Overall very good start.

entry.title: Re: How to make a field editable after payment is posted
entry.content:
Hello Branislav,

I hope you are doing well .

Directly configuring the ISR/QR Ref number fields to be editable after posting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is not a standard configuration option and is generally not supported or recommended due to the critical nature of financial data integrity and auditability, especially concerning payment references.

Your current process of reversing and re-entering is the typical and most compliant way to correct such errors in a posted financial document in SAP. The best approach is to focus on preventing these errors from occurring in the first place through improved processes, validations, and user training. If the current reversal process is excessively burdensome, investigate the root causes of the errors to minimize their frequency.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3512188

Best regards

Fabio

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Kudos to the UIx team! Navigating the SAP resource pages now feels seamless and intuitive. The user interface shows a noticeable improvement in engagement, visual appeal, and structural layout across various sections.

The Community page, in particular, stands out — it’s visually engaging, easy to navigate, and a real time-saver when searching for information or exploring new ideas. Overall, it creates a more motivating, new dimension and supportive environment for learning!

Rac

entry.title: Re: Vida de Gerente de Projetos: Metodologia SAP Activate
entry.content: Muito bom!

entry.title: Re: EDIT_MODE in REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY
entry.content: Thanks that helped me today 🙂 . I was missing LC_GLAY-EDT_CLL_CB = 'X'.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM website does not open and produce an error: To...
entry.content:
Hi Ryno,

Just to document this issue, in case other users ever find this.

In case of a "Too many redirects 302" always first check the DM application server log, in C:\inetpub\wwwroot\SAP\SAP Disclosure Management\Logs\AppServer

Quite often, the issue is due to a DB connection issue (e.g. password failure)

In the log, you would see:

Login failed for user 'CDMUser'

This can be fixed by changing the DB connection string, see: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/cpm/5277047791.html

 

Best regards

Marc

Thanks,

Sampada.

entry.title: Interview Scheduling Outlook
entry.content:
Hi Experts

Can you please suggest. Please refer error facing as below:

Server Connection Failed

Availability could not be found for these Interviewers: RCM ADMIN.
Please try the following recommendations and try again
  • Check with your Administrator to verify Outlook Integration configuration is correct.
  • Verify that the interviewer’s Employee Profile email address is correct.
  • Verify that the interviewer’s Default Calendar Permissions are "Free/Busy time" or that the Service Account has at least this level of access.
  • Try again later.

entry.title: How to set EWM movement Reason into /SCWM/IF_EX_ERP_GOODSMVT~CHANGE_MATDOC
entry.content:
Hi experts

we are using BADI /SCWM/IF_EX_ERP_GOODSMVT~CHANGE_MATDOC to convert movement Reason  from EWM into ERP.
To my mind   i can  find the EWM movement reason  in field REASON in parameter IT_ORDIM_C . but when i debug the BADI. the Reason FIELD is blank . Also I go  through all other parameters and can not find the movement Reason.
Can anyone share me How can i carry the EWM Reason  into BADI parameter ?

entry.title: SAP B1 Crystal Report
entry.content:
Can anyone guide me on how to make my Crystal Report layout dynamically expand to additional pages based on the amount of text in a field? In my Purchase Order form, I have a UDF field for Terms and Conditions, which can contain a long text input from the user. However, when I place this UDF in the Report Footer, nothing is displayed if the text is too long.

What I want is for the Terms and Conditions to display properly even if they are very long and to continue onto the next page(s) if needed. Ideally, I also want page numbers to reflect this.

Please help me figure this out. I’ve attached a visual to better explain my issue.

entry.title: SAC Dashboard 'Variance %' Threshold values color should appear on 'Actual' Value
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement of Bar chart with Actual,  plan and variance %.

In the SAC  Dashboard, the 'Variance %' threshold color is intended to appear on the 'Actual' value, not on the variance itself.

Variance in threshold values which changes the color dynamically based on % values.

GE = 0  'Green'

 0 to -0.2 ' yellow'

Less then -0.2 ' Red's

As shown in the below Pic.  'Ava Power Variance' threshold color should appear same color on 'Availability Power - AYTD%'.

Please suggest how we can change the 'Actual' color dynamically based on  "Variance %'  Threshold Colors.  

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Identity Directory API specification is broken
entry.content:
Hi @NickVA

is it possible to share the file with me. 

Thank You.
Viveka

entry.title: What exactly is SAP BW PCE… and what it is not !
entry.content:
Dear BW enthusiasts!
In February this year, SAP announced SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). Amongst others, it combines the strengths of SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud and provides many innovations including a deep integration with SAP Databricks for ML and AI use cases. In addition, the term “BW Modernization” also plays a key role in this new environment.

This blog post is meant to clarify the product behind this term, which is sometimes referred to SAP BW[4] PCE. Other terms like RISE-BW, BW cloud private edition, BW tailored option, BW BDC, SAP BW NetWeaver Cloud private edition, or SAP Business Warehouse Cloud are sometimes used in this context as well and that altogether leads to a high level of confusion. So, if you feel the same – keep on reading…

So let’s start to define the term exactly: What is this SAP BW[4] PCE ?
PCE means private cloud edition and refers to a deployment in the SAP private cloud as opposed to on-premise deployments or deployments at hosting/service partners.
BW[4] is an artificial umbrella term we at SAP like to use referring to both products equally:  SAP NetWeaver BW 7.5 on HANA and SAP BW/4HANA. You surely know that both products are quite different; but here the focus is not on features&functions, but on the private cloud deployment mode.
So, the image above means, that you are on SAP´s recommended BW modernization path if you run SAP BW 7.5 on HANA or SAP BW/4HANA in SAP´s private cloud.

What is SAP BW[4] PTO?
PTO stands for private cloud tailored option and refers to infrastructure configuration in the private cloud deployment, e.g. CPU power, memory size, disc space, disaster recovnery&backup concepts, SLAs just name some typical ones.
In standard PCE terms, this configuration is defined by the well-known T-shirt size approach concept. But if customer requirements cannot be covered there, a more specific approach can be designed in a “tailored option”. So in a nutshell, PTO is a dedicated flavor of PCE to meet individual customer needs regarding infrastructure.
In this blog and many other sources, the term PCE includes PTO flavors as we focus on the deployment in the private cloud only irrespective of infrastructure details. So if you read SAP BW PCE, a the system might run also with the tailored option.

What is SAP BW[4] PCE not?
Very clearly – SAP BW[4] PCE is not a new or revised product. It is not a new codeline.
It is the same codeline as BW on HANA or BW/4HANA on premise.
Also worth mentioning - it is not a public cloud solution.
Finally, it does not restrict any existing features. In terms of functionality SAP BW 7.5 on HANA  PCE provides 100% of the features of SAP BW 7.5 on HANA. And SAP BW/4HANA covers the exact same scope as SAP BW/4HANA PCE of the same release and SP level. 
Exception: The so-called Data Product Generator is available in PCE depoyments in BDC only – but what you currently use in SAP BW[4] on-premise is also available in BW[4] PCE. 

How to get to SAP BW[4] PCE?
If your BW system is managed by SAP already, then you are there already: You leverage BW[4] PCE.
For all other customers: a technical migration is needed from the current data center to a Hyperscaler data center managed by SAP. This migration is sometimes referred to as “Lift&Shift”, because in most cases it is a 1:1 move without any changes to the database or the ABAP application stack. It comes with a downtime for each system tier and some testing effort in similar scale as a SP upgrade of course, but expectation is generally that everything keeps working in PCE in a non-disruptive way.
If the system is currently running on a 3rd party database, a migration to SAP HANA is required, because PCE´s minimal requirements are BW 7.5 on HANA. You can also include a ABAP stack update or upgrade when moving to PCE.

SAP provides the RISE methodology including tools, concepts, processes, experts etc. to bring customer SAP-landscape into PCE. So, if customers sometimes refer to their “SAP RISE BW”, but actually mean BW[4] deployed in PCE after a successful RISE project delivered by SAP.

What is different with SAP BW[4] PCE in SAP Business Data Cloud?
If your SAP BW[4] PCE subscription includes SAP BDC capacity units, you have currently one additional feature: The Data Product Generator which derives data products from SAP BW[4] PCE InfoProviders into the HANA object store of SAP Datasphere. Based on this integration, customers can redesign relevant applications in SAP Datasphere + SAP Analytics Cloud step-by-step. SAP note 3590400 explains how to enable this additional function and SAP note 3584640 explains, that embedded BW scenarios are out of scope in general. Also refer to SAP BW/4HANA help portal.

What about system maintenance end dates?
One important value added of running BW 7.5 in PCE is the automatic maintenance extension of three years until end of 2030 with no additional fees. See SAP note 3590297 and the SAP Product Availability Matrix (PAM) for more details.

Summary
The purpose of this blog post is to explain the significance of a private cloud deployment for SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA. We at SAP see this as the first crucial step towards a viable modernization shifting from classic BW data flows and BW queries towards Datasphere views and SAC reporting&planning. There are still open questions regarding the detailed modernization approach, and future publication will surely follow...
However, it should be clear, that the move into PCE based on a RISE migration project is a step into the right direction which finally ends in SAP Business Data Cloud including BW[4] PCE. In a nutshell:

  1. Go through a RISE project to bring your BW landscape to an SAP PCE deployment.
  2. Then, enhance your PCE subscription commercially to SAP Business Data Cloud and expand/shift BW scenarios to SAP Datasphere + SAP Analytics Cloud step by step.
  3. Finally adopt SAP BDC completely and decommission SAP BW[4] PCE.

Let´s also emphasize once more, that we are talking of one technical migration only. Shifting from PCE to BDC is mainly a commercial adjustment but does not result in a costly or complex technical migration.

Some useful notes

  • 0052505 - Support after end of mainstream maintenance or extended maintenance
  • 1648480 - Maintenance for SAP Business Suite 7 including SAP NetWeaver
  • 2741041 - Maintenance for SAP NetWeaver 7.x Business Warehouse
  • 2934895 - Maintenance for SAP BW/4HANA
  • 3590297 - Information about maintenance availability in SAP BW private cloud editions
  • 3590400 - Configuration SAP BW[4]: Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud
  • 3584640 - Data Product Generator in embedded SAP BW in SAP ERP and S/4HANA
  • SAP BW/4HANA help > Integrating Data from the Data Product Generator for SAP Business Data Cloud

Thanks to Alex for sharing his thoughts...

entry.title: Re: report painter: add a column related to balance carry...
entry.content: i'm still not getting result, any other suggestion please, thanks

entry.title: Re: MDG Consolidation using BP SOA service
entry.content: It's been a while that this question was posted but unfortunately no answer or guidance received yet...let me rephrase the question one more time. can anybody share some detailed steps of what needs to be done referring to this SAP help ...I am desparately looking for a help here.. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MASTER_DATA_GOVERNANCE/6b8255b03aa44628880b5b71c16ddb91/dbc19b55f22d7b43e10000000a4450e5.html

entry.title: SAP C4C SDK 2505: New Visual Studio 2022 based Setup
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

Has anyone successfully installed ^ worked the new Visual Studio 2022 based SAP Cloud Application Studio setup on a Intel machine?

I have installed "Visual Studio 2022" Enterprise Edition and then SDK extension from me.sap.com.

Now when I create a new project then I am able ot get the pop up to enter my credentials(FYI...You will get an error at this step if you use free community edition).

However, after entering the credentials, i get an error "Value Cannot be NULL" exception in Visual Studio.

I have followed all the instructions as in https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_APPLICATIONS_STUDIO/cbcebe3cfb1647a8b0322c18dbb0b481/d5278bebf61f4018b7468d669ea3a231.html?locale=en-US

Any help/input is really appriciated!!!

Thanks

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: node rfc server issue being unreliable
entry.content:
Strange, looks fine so far.

In this case, there must be something very wrong either in the Python wrapper around libsapnwrfc.so, or in your Python code itself. Try setting the environment variable RFC_TRACE=4, before starting the server program. Then we can see, what the program is doing.

I have never used Python, but when doing it in C, I would do something like

RFC_ERROR_INFO errorInfo;
// Either manually created or obtained from ABAP DDIC:
RFC_FUNCTION_DESC_HANDLE myFuncDesc = ...;

RfcInstallServerFunction(cU("NPL"), myFuncDesc, handlerFunction, &errorInfo);

at the very beginning, and the function should be known for the runtime of the process. The only way to make it "unknown" again, is to call RfcInstallServerFunction a second time like this

RfcInstallServerFunction(cU("NPL"), myFuncDesc, NULL, &errorInfo);

If this is happening, we should see it in the RFC Trace.

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content: All documentation in the SAP Business One space stopped working:

entry.title: Display Original Duplicate Triplicate on Delivery challan output form
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working with Adobe Forms in S/4HANA Public Cloud, we developed delivery challan output form in such a way that

when we click on preview we have to get four copies . It was done.

And now client requirement is we have to display as

 

  • Original on first copy

  • Duplicate on second copy

  • Triplicate on third copy

  • Extra Copy on fourth copy

Note :  I have placed header and footer part in master page.

How can i do this . please guide me.

Thanks

Sarat.
